removed.title: `docs/operational-disciplines.md`
removed.body: (60 lines), **`docs/implementation-disciplines.md`** (68 lines), **`docs/user-surface-disciplines.md`** (67 lines) — three sibling per-surface discipline files merged into `docs/situational-disciplines.md`. Section bodies preserved verbatim under three stable anchors (`#user-surface`, `#implementation-surface`, `#operational-surface`); cross-cutting concerns (security / performance / observability / testability / error handling / build-time risk) remain in [`docs/cross-cutting-concerns.md`](docs/cross-cutting-concerns.md) and are not duplicated. Five active consumers updated: `docs/principles.md` line 111, `docs/README.md` Tier 3 table, `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` §Per-surface disciplines, `docs/adoption-anti-metrics.md` §See also, `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml §handoff_narrative` description (regenerated `schemas/change-manifest.schema.json` in lockstep). Historical `ROADMAP.md` references in P2 (lines 113, 120) describe past audit work and are preserved verbatim per `CLAUDE.md §3`.

changed.title: `reference-implementations/validator-node/` → `reference-implementations/community/validator-node/`
changed.body: moved verbatim with all source, tests, fixtures, package.json, and tsconfig.json intact. The implementation is preserved for users who already depend on it; rule parity with `validator-python/` is no longer guaranteed by this repository's CI. README header rewritten to declare community-maintained status; relative paths updated for the new depth (`../validator-python/` → `../../validator-python/`; `../../docs/` → `../../../docs/`; CLI snippets `../../schemas/` → `../../../schemas/`). All five active cite-by-name consumers updated: `AGENTS.md §File role map` (now distinguishes first-class `validator-{posix-shell,python}/` from community `community/validator-node/`), `AGENTS.md §Reference implementations` bullet, `README.md §Repository layout` directory tree, `README.md §When to look here` validator pointer, `reference-implementations/re-entry-trigger/README.md §Integration with validators`, `reference-implementations/INVENTORY.md` (new §Validator classes section replaces the §Validator parity note added in 1.19.1).

changed.title: `reference-implementations/INVENTORY.md`
changed.body: new §Validator classes section replaces the 1.19.1 §Validator parity note. First-class references (`validator-posix-shell`, `validator-python`) and the community-maintained reference (`community/validator-node`) are now listed in separate tables with their respective trade-offs; total file count breakdown updated to reflect first-class (114) vs community-maintained (25) split.

added.title: `docs/situational-disciplines.md`
added.body: the merged per-surface disciplines file. Three sections (`#user-surface`, `#implementation-surface`, `#operational-surface`) with stable anchors. Top-of-file TL;DR explains the merge structure and routes readers to the section that matches their change. Each section keeps its own "When this section applies" trigger list, observation axes, and review checklist. The activate-only-what-applies routing principle from the original three files is preserved by keeping the sections independently readable.

notes.title: External consumers citing the three deleted disciplines files by exact path must update

notes.body: Update their CI / scripts to `reference-implementations/community/validator-node/`; the implementation is byte-identical at the time of the move. The community label means future rule-semantics changes in `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md` may not be reflected here, so adopters should run the bundled `tests/` against new spec versions.
Migrate to `reference-implementations/validator-python/` (the first-class language-native reference). The two were declared functionally equivalent in 1.19.1; fixtures are byte-for-byte equal.

notes.title: No code migration required
notes.body: for users of `validator-posix-shell/` or `validator-python/`; those remain unchanged.

notes.title: Schema description string change
notes.body: (`handoff_narrative.description` in `change-manifest.schema.yaml`) only updates the doc-pointer URL within the description; no field semantics change. Existing manifests validate identically.

removed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/core-principles.md`
removed.body: (10 lines) — content was a subset of `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md §Core operating principles` (the SKILL.md version covers 10 principles vs the stub's 8). The "Quick refresher" pointer in SKILL.md is dropped; the surviving cross-reference in `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` line 11 now points at `SKILL.md §Core operating principles` instead of the deleted stub.

removed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/cross-cutting-quick-check.md`
removed.body: (10 lines) — content was a subset of `SKILL.md §Cross-cutting rule`. SKILL.md absorbed the stub's "error handling" cross-cutting concern (it previously listed only Security / Performance / Observability / Testability) and the explicit prompts under each item. The two "Quick reference" pointers to the stub are removed.

removed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase8-trigger-guide.md`
removed.body: (11 lines) — content was a subset of `docs/post-delivery-observation.md §Phase 8 is not mandatory`. Five cross-references repointed at the canonical SoT: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` line 393, `docs/phase-command-vocabulary.md` line 60, `skills/engineering-workflow/references/long-running-delegation.md` lines 24 / 157 / 215. SKILL.md §Phase 8 trigger pointer is now `docs/post-delivery-observation.md` directly.

removed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/debugging-process.md`
removed.body: (10 lines) — content was inlined into `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase4-implement.md` as a new `## Built-in debugging process` section between `## Built-in steps` and `## Rules`. The stub's "Supplement" pointer in SKILL.md §Phase 4 is dropped; the SKILL.md description now notes the debugging process is included in `phase4-implement.md`.

added.title: `docs/examples/README.md`
added.body: routing index for the 16 worked examples under `docs/examples/`. Three tables route by **scenario type** (generic / bugfix / refactor / migration / mobile-offline), **domain** (game-dev / live-ops / ML / data-pipeline / firmware), and **stack** (Flutter / Android-Kotlin / iOS-Swift / React-Next.js / Ktor / Unity, each linked to its bridge under `docs/bridges/`). A trailing section cross-references the six worked-example manifests under `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/manifests/`. Closes the discoverability gap identified by the audit: previously a reader opening `docs/examples/` saw 16 sibling files with no priority signal.

added.title: `reference-implementations/INVENTORY.md` §Validator parity note (1.19.1)
added.body: explicit declaration that `validator-python/` and `validator-node/` are functionally equivalent parallel implementations (same rule-ID coverage, same exit-code contract, same default cache-file shape, fixtures byte-for-byte equal). Adopters need only one. A picker table contrasts the two by ecosystem fit (Python/ML vs JS/TS/web) and runtime cost (~400 ms vs ~200 ms). `validator-posix-shell/` is explicitly noted as covering a different niche (zero-runtime-dep portability with the documented Layer-2 / Layer-3 coverage gap).

added.title: `CONTRIBUTING.md` §CHANGELOG entry style
added.body: codifies the entry style implicit in 1.16.0 onward. Required sections (`Added` / `Changed` / `Removed` / `Fixed`) plus conditional-only sections (`Why minor / patch`, `Tool-agnostic discipline`, `Migration notes`, `Bootstrap exception`). `Why minor / patch` capped at 4 lines. Historical entries remain factual records per `CLAUDE.md §3` — the style guide applies to new entries only.

changed.title: `docs/change-manifest-spec.md` restructured + new companion `docs/change-manifest-spec-cookbook.md`
changed.body: (M3 / P4a, **L1+**) — extracted three sections from the spec into a non-normative companion file: §CI integration (recipes), §Modes of AI use of the manifest (generation / validation / hand-off), §Mission-shaped manifests (extended pattern), and §Example tour (six worked manifests). The spec itself loses 79 lines (911 → 832); each extracted section is replaced in the spec by a one-paragraph pointer to the cookbook so existing readers reach the same material via one extra link. The cookbook is **non-normative** by construction — if a recipe ever contradicts the spec, the spec wins. No section anchor was deleted from the spec; the spec retains its canonical role and the cookbook is additive. Drives a measurable reduction in LLM-context cost for the most-cited spec in the repo.

changed.title: `docs/source-of-truth-patterns.md` restructured + new companion `docs/sot-desync-anti-patterns.md`
changed.body: (M3 / P4b, **L1+**) — extracted §Common desync anti-patterns (six anti-patterns) and §Standard strategies for repairing desync (four strategies) from the SoT-patterns spec into a diagnostic appendix file. Net reduction in the spec: 115 lines (695 → 580, 16.5% smaller). The appendix adds a new mapping table from each anti-pattern to its most-common repair strategy; the mapping is descriptive, not prescriptive — no new normative rule. The split is by purpose (identification vs diagnosis) so readers identifying their SoT pattern stay in one file; readers assessing desync risk or designing a repair reach for the appendix.

**`templates/change-manifest.example-*.yaml` moved to `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/manifests/`** (M3 / P8, **L1+**) — the six worked-example manifests (CRUD / mobile-offline / game-gacha / mission-evaluator / multi-agent-handoff / security-sensitive) now live alongside the other engineering-workflow templates instead of at the repo root. Updated all consumers: `README.md` §4 quadrant + the two specific links; `AGENTS.md` §Manifest section + worked-example list; `GEMINI.md` §Templates pointer; `docs/change-manifest-spec.md` (4 references); `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` §496 pointer; `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` §security-supply-chain example pointer; `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug.md` checklist; `.github/scripts/validate-templates.py` glob and docstring; `.github/scripts/validate-cluster-disjointness.py` glob and docstring. Historical CHANGELOG entries are preserved verbatim per `CLAUDE.md §3` (the old `templates/` paths in the 1.14.5 release notes are factual records and remain unchanged). The repo-root `templates/` directory is removed; new manifests for users belong under `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/manifests/`.

added.title: `docs/change-manifest-spec-cookbook.md`
added.body: (M3 / P4a) — non-normative companion to the manifest spec covering CI-integration recipes, AI usage modes (generation / validation / hand-off), the mission-shaped-manifest pattern, and a tour of the six worked example templates.

added.title: `docs/sot-desync-anti-patterns.md`
added.body: (M3 / P4b) — diagnostic appendix to `source-of-truth-patterns.md` covering the six common desync anti-patterns, the four standard repair strategies, and a mapping table from anti-pattern to most-common repair.

added.title: `docs/README.md`
added.body: (M2) — hand-curated 4-tier index of the methodology layer (Tier 1 onboarding / Tier 2 core contract / Tier 3 disciplines / Tier 4 references). Closes the navigability gap the repo audit identified: previously, a new contributor opening `docs/` saw 43 same-level files with no priority. The index treats each existing doc as already-classified by its filename and section role, so no per-file frontmatter is required (frontmatter + auto-generated index are deferred to a future Change Manifest). Includes a "How to extend" section that codifies which tier new docs belong to and why renames are breaking-change events for cross-references (cites the file-naming rule added to `CONTRIBUTING.md`).

added.title: `docs/decision-trees.md`
added.body: (M2) — single-page hub for the three most-asked routing decisions: (Tree A) *Need a Change Manifest?*, (Tree B) *Which SoT pattern (1–10 + 4a)?*, (Tree C) *Single-agent or multi-agent role split?*. Each tree gives a 30–60 second fast call and routes to the canonical source (`mode-decision-tree.md` for force-Full triggers; `source-of-truth-patterns.md` for full pattern catalogue; `multi-agent-handoff.md` for tool-permission matrix). Status is non-normative routing aid; canonical sources remain binding. Quick-aid pointers added to `glossary.md`, `source-of-truth-patterns.md`, and `mode-decision-tree.md` so users searching definition / pattern / mode pages discover the decision hub. The pointers are additive — no existing cross-link is removed.

added.title: `.cursor/rules/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.mdc` thinning
added.body: (M2) — replaced the duplicated "Inputs" / "Responsibilities" prose (which previously restated the same 5–8 step checklist found in `reference-implementations/roles/*.md`) with a one-paragraph pointer to the canonical paste-ready prompt under `reference-implementations/roles/`, citing the `role-consistency` CI job that holds them in sync. Cursor-specific glue (Custom Mode wiring, "When this rule is active", anti-collusion via fresh session) and the irreducible role boundaries / anti-collusion sections are preserved verbatim. Net reduction: 22 lines across the three files; `role-consistency` CI verified green after the change.

added.title: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` `role-consistency` job + `.github/scripts/check-role-consistency.py`
added.body: (M1) — new gate that verifies the multi-agent role contract stays consistent between its SoT (`docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` §Tool-permission matrix + §Single-agent anti-collusion rule) and the nine runtime mirrors that restate it (`agents/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.md`, `.cursor/rules/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.mdc`, `reference-implementations/roles/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.md`). The checker validates phrasing-flexible **semantic invariants** (Planner declares no write/edit; Implementer declares no self-review and no surface re-classification; Reviewer declares no edit/write — the single most important row — plus anti-collusion) rather than byte-equality, since each mirror is intentionally tailored to its runtime's enforcement mechanism. Closes a structural gap noted by repo audit: a SoT change in §7 previously required hand-syncing 9 mirror files with no CI to catch a missed propagation. Self-test covers 5 fixture cases including the SoT-reference invariant being correctly skipped when checking the SoT itself (avoiding self-reference paradox).

added.title: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` `internal-links` job + `.github/scripts/check-internal-links.py`
added.body: (M1) — new offline link-integrity gate that fails the build if any markdown file contains a broken relative link. Scope is deliberately narrow to keep CI offline-safe: inline `[text](target)` and reference-style `[text]: target` syntax for relative paths only; `http(s)://`, `mailto:`, `tel:`, `data:`, and intra-document `#anchor`-only links are skipped. Anchor fragments are stripped before path resolution (file-existence verified, not anchor-existence — anchor-level validation is a future enhancement that requires markdown header parsing). Path-traversal targets that escape repo root are reported as violations. Self-test covers 8 fixture cases including ref-style links, traversal escape, inline-code-span examples, and fenced-code-block examples (the last two are regression cases for the bugfix described in M2). The repo's now-163 markdown files all currently pass; the gate prevents regressions during file moves and renames.

added.title: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` `legacy-terms` job + `.github/scripts/check-legacy-terms.py`
added.body: (M1) — new gate that blocks re-introduction of vocabulary retired in `docs/glossary.md §Note on legacy names` (`no-process path`, `ultra-lean`, `No skill`, `three-line handoff`, `stripped-down version`). Allow-list covers exactly the files where these terms legitimately appear: the glossary itself (which defines the retirements), `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md` (which cross-references the retired aliases in the same prose convention), and the historical record (`CHANGELOG{,.archive}.{md,json}`, `ROADMAP{,.archive}.md`, kept verbatim per `CLAUDE.md §3 "Do not rewrite historical CHANGELOG entries"`). Implementation uses word-boundary regex (`(?<!\w)term(?!\w)`) so partial-word matches like "ultra-leanness" do not trigger; self-test covers 5 cases including the boundary edge.

added.title: `reference-implementations/INVENTORY.md`
added.body: (M1) — non-normative inventory file that classifies each entry under `reference-implementations/` as either **runtime-glue** (executable code or runtime-specific configuration that must live outside `docs/`: hook scripts, validator source, selftest fixtures, runtime adapter glue — 139 files across 9 directories) or **documentation** (prose mirroring normative content, currently kept here because it ships paste-ready to runtimes that cannot consume `docs/` directly — 7 files inside `roles/`). The classification supports a future Change Manifest that may relocate selected `documentation`-class files into `docs/`; this file deliberately does not perform the relocation, only the inventory. Not cited from `docs/`; consumers are reviewers and audit tooling.

added.title: `CONTRIBUTING.md` §File-naming conventions
added.body: (M1) — codifies the kebab-case + head-noun-last rule already in use across `docs/` and `skills/engineering-workflow/references/`, plus the rule that filename renames invalidate cross-references and therefore require a Change Manifest (referencing `docs/change-manifest-spec.md`). No existing file is renamed; the section makes the convention explicit so new contributors do not introduce drift, and codifies the trade-off that prevented a proposed rename of `cluster-parallelism.md` (semantically already consistent with `phase-overlap-zones.md` under the head-noun-last rule).

fixed.title: `.github/scripts/check-internal-links.py`
fixed.body: (M2) — strip fenced code blocks and inline code spans (1–3 backtick spans) before extracting links, so `[text](target)` syntax inside backticks is not chased as a real link. The bug was triggered by the M1 CHANGELOG entry that contained literal `[text](target)` syntax in code spans. Two regression cases added to the self-test (case 7 inline code, case 8 fenced block); now 8 cases.

fixed.title: Pre-PR audit cleanup
fixed.body: independent audit before opening the release PR caught seven stale references to the now-deleted repo-root `templates/` directory (in `README.md` §Custom agent + directory tree, `AGENTS.md` §What you get + §File role map, `CLAUDE.md` §Default to documentation-type edits, `reference-implementations/README.md` §What does NOT belong here, `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`); each updated to point at the new `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/manifests/` location or removed if redundant. Also added inbound discoverability links so the M2 new files are not orphaned: `docs/README.md` is now linked from `README.md` §Reading paths and `AGENTS.md` §File role map; `docs/decision-trees.md` is linked from `README.md` §30-second lookup and `docs/onboarding/orientation.md`; `reference-implementations/INVENTORY.md` is linked from `reference-implementations/README.md` and `AGENTS.md` §File role map. Confirmed via `internal-links` CI gate (160 → 163 markdown files, all resolve).

fixed.title: `.github/scripts/check-internal-links.py` (image-link support)
fixed.body: extended the inline-link regex to match both `[text](target)` and `![alt](target)`, so broken image-link targets (rare in this repo today, but possible in future bridge / diagram additions) are reported. Added self-test case 9 covering a broken image link; now 9 cases.

fixed.title: `.github/scripts/check-role-consistency.py` (defensive SoT-not-found)
fixed.body: added an early existence check for `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` before iterating invariants. Without it, a future rename of the SoT file would cause every invariant to silently fail with "missing invariant 'X' (planner): must reference docs/multi-agent-handoff.md as SoT", obscuring the root cause. Now reports `FAIL SoT file not found: docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` with a hint to update `SOT_PATH` and search for old path consumers.

changed.title: `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/hooks/sot-drift-check.sh` and `consumer-registry-check.sh`
changed.body: replaced unquoted `for X in $list` iteration of yq output with `yq … | while IFS= read -r X` pipelines, capturing emitted warnings into a single variable so the warn-counter survives the pipeline subshell. Eliminates word-splitting and pathname-expansion hazards if a manifest declares a SoT source path or `external_registry_url` containing whitespace or glob metacharacters. Behavior preserved: `consumer-registry-check.sh` still routes the "non-HTTP URL skipped" notice to stderr only (does not promote it to a warning); both hooks still exit 2 on any real warning. Verified against the existing fixture suite (`pass-no-consumers`, `pass-reachable`, `warn-unreachable`, `pass-sot-touched`, `warn-sot-untouched`).

changed.title: `reference-implementations/hooks-{codex,cursor,gemini-cli,windsurf}/selftests/selftest.sh`
changed.body: removed `eval "export $key=\"\$val\""` from the test-input loop; replaced with the POSIX-portable `export "$key=$val"` (which all reference shells — sh, bash, dash, mksh, busybox ash — accept directly). Trust boundary unchanged: callers are hardcoded literals (`"CURSOR_HOOK_TRIGGER=pre-commit"` etc.), so this was not exploitable in practice, but the prior pattern propagated a `key`-not-quoted footgun into a reference implementation that other workspaces copy from.

fixed.title: `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/hooks/evidence-artifact-exists.sh`
fixed.body: removed two unused locals (`missing=""`, `empty_location=""`, lines 29–30) flagged by `shellcheck` as SC2034. Dead since the hook switched to inline `printf … >&2; exit 1` reporting; their persistence served no purpose and would have masked accidental-aliasing bugs in any future edit that re-introduced them.

added.title: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` `hooks-shellcheck` job
added.body: new lint gate that runs `shellcheck -s sh` on every `.sh` file under `reference-implementations/hooks-{claude-code,codex,cursor,gemini-cli,windsurf}/{hooks,adapter,selftests}`. Made a prerequisite of `hooks-selftest` (`needs: hooks-shellcheck`) so a lint failure short-circuits the slower selftest job. This is the deterministic second-line defense the `runtime-hook-contract.md` security clauses imply but did not previously enforce: `shellcheck` mechanically catches the heredoc-without-single-quotes / unquoted-`$var` / unquoted-word-splitting / `eval` patterns that classify as code-injection footguns when input data flows through them. Scope is intentionally narrow — the runtime-hook surface itself; `reference-implementations/validator-posix-shell/` has separate pre-existing findings (SC3012 non-portable `\<` in POSIX `[`, SC2015 `A && B || C` idiom usage) that are out of scope for this hook-security pass and remain unlinted by this job.

Inline `# shellcheck disable=SC1007` directives on the `CDPATH= cd …` POSIX-CDPATH-neutralization idiom (selftests in 5 runtimes), and `# shellcheck source=/dev/null` directives on the runtime-resolved `. "$adapter"` source statement (4 adapter selftests). Both patterns are intentional; the directives document the intent so CI does not silence legitimate future findings via a project-wide ignore.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` §Three canonical roles — non-normative rationale paragraph
added.body: one paragraph after the "responsibility, not by identity" line spelling out why the role split is by what each role *does* + which tool envelope enforces it, rather than by which human-organization category the role nominally resembles. Names the one-way "PM → RD → QA" knowledge-bottleneck failure mode the existing contract is designed to break, and points at three already-canonical mechanisms that prevent it — every downstream role reads every upstream artifact (no compression at the boundary), differential write permission per Reviewer §Must not do (audit value mechanical rather than trust-based), and three-tier `§Conflict resolution` reopens the upstream phase rather than letting scope shifts get overwritten silently. Cited anchors verified to exist (`§Conflict resolution` line 337; Reviewer `§Must not do` line 123). The paragraph is explanatory only — it cites existing rules in surrounding sections rather than introducing new ones.

added.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/long-running-delegation.md`
added.body: new execution-layer reference naming the time-axis discipline for any sub-agent invocation whose expected runtime exceeds one prompt-cache window (commonly ~5 minutes). Three disciplines: **D1 Checkpoint-bounded delegation** (single-invocation scope must fit within one cache window; long scope is split into reviewable segments; lower bound ~2 minutes per segment to avoid over-decomposition), **D2 Artifact-grounded progress** (sub-agent writes append-only structured progress to a session-scoped Rule-5a path known to the canonical role at spawn time; last progress entry is the failure-signalling primary source; canonical writes only after segment return), **D3 Canonical-role non-idle rule** (canonical role must have defined concurrent work or a polling plan during the delegation — allowed: D2-tail / context-pack pre-distill / named overlap-zone prep / independent batched fan-out; prohibited: canonical-role work-ahead per `phase-overlap-zones.md §Rule 1` / mid-flight manifest writes per `phase-gate-discipline.md §Rule 5a` / polling-by-re-invocation per `role-composition-patterns.md §Invocation lifecycle`). D3 is the within-delegation analog of `docs/ai-operating-contract.md §11` (narration is not action) — §11 closes the pre-tool-call silence gap; D3 closes the post-tool-call silence gap; neither subsumes the other. Full mode only (sub-agent composition is Full-only per `role-composition-patterns.md §When`). Tool-agnostic capability-category mapping names **background spawn**, **working-space artifact read**, **sub-agent progress write**, and **addressable sub-agent (optional)**; explicit fallback behavior when a runtime lacks background spawn (D1 remains, D3 collapses) or lacks addressable sub-agent (D3 checkpoint-only). Three existing named scenarios are documented as specific instances of the general rule — Phase 8 observer (`phase8-trigger-guide.md` + `docs/post-delivery-observation.md`), Pattern C cluster halt-all (`cluster-parallelism.md §4`), and Reviewer Tier-1 send-back (`docs/multi-agent-handoff.md §Conflict resolution`).

added.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/mode-decision-tree.md §Scenarios that force Full`
added.body: new row **Canonical methodology content edit (L1+)** closing a self-referential gap: for this tool-agnostic methodology-plugin repo whose "product" IS the methodology, editing canonical SoT content (files listed as canonical per `AGENTS.md §File role map`: `AGENTS.md`, `docs/*.md`, `schemas/*`, canonical `skills/**` files) at breaking-change level ≥ L1 — changing an existing normative claim, adding a new normative rule to SoT, or renaming a cross-cutting term — forces Full. Purely additive L0 edits (new non-normative reference file; pointer additions; CHANGELOG-only edits) remain Lean-eligible. Prior state: the forced-Full table was phrased in product-engineering terms only (DB migration / public API / enum / money / auth / PII / cross-team / rollout); the equivalence "editing the methodology's own SoT structurally equals Public API breaking change" was not spelled out.

added.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/discovery-loop.md §Mode upgrade trigger`
added.body: new mandatory-upgrade Criterion bullet: *"If the new findings require changing an existing normative claim in canonical methodology content (L1+ canonical edit per `mode-decision-tree.md §Scenarios that force Full`) → upgrade is mandatory."* Consistent with the new forced-Full row.

added.title: Pointer updates
added.body: across six consumer files pointing at the new `long-running-delegation.md` reference: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Quick-refresher block (one-liner with Full-mode-only caveat), `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/subagent-strategy.md` new §Long-running invocations closing section (parallel to the existing §Within-phase fan-out vs between-phase overlap), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/parallelization-patterns.md §Relationship to other documents` (sibling-rule framing: spawn timing vs single-invocation runtime scope), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/cluster-parallelism.md §Relation to other documents` (Pattern C Planner's cluster-status monitoring as a D3 instance; D1 applies when a cluster's scope would exceed one cache window), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase-overlap-zones.md §Relation to other rules` (D3's allowed-concurrent-work list respects Rule 1; overlap prep into an unnamed zone is still a violation regardless of whether a sub-agent is running).

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/mode-decision-tree.md` intro paragraph
changed.body: tightened from *"The decision is not permanent — if scope grows during implementation, upgrade"* to the composite rule: *"Default bias is the lightest mode the scenario tables allow; upgrade on the spot when in-flight discovery reveals a forced-trigger that was not visible at mode-pick time; do not force-fit a lighter mode to hide scope that has already grown — silent scope-shrink is prohibited per `AGENTS.md §6` and `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md §Rule 1`."* Prior state: the three mechanisms (default-lightest / upgrade-on-discovery / silent-scope-shrink-prohibited) were each normative but distributed across five files — `mode-decision-tree.md §Mode upgrade / downgrade`, `misuse-signals.md §Misuse 2`, `discovery-loop.md §Mode upgrade trigger`, `ai-operating-contract.md §2` + `§5`, `phase-gate-discipline.md:52`, and `AGENTS.md:71`. A reader picking a mode had to reconstruct the composite from five reads. The new intro is the single findable statement; the distributed rules remain authoritative on each mechanism's details.

changed.title: `CLAUDE.md §5`
changed.body: added a `**Mode implication.**` paragraph connecting the existing cross-cutting-term discipline (*"find and update all consumers"*) to the mode-selection consequence: a cross-cutting-term edit at breaking-change level ≥ L1 is a forced-Full trigger (points at the new `mode-decision-tree.md` row); L0-additive edits remain Lean-eligible. Default lightest / upgrade on discovery is re-stated here so a contributor reading this repo-specific bridge file does not need to open two canonical files to understand the mode consequence.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/misuse-signals.md §Misuse 2`
changed.body: added a Signal bullet recognizing the canonical-edit misuse case explicitly: *"A canonical methodology content edit at breaking-change level ≥ L1 is being routed through Lean or below. Purely additive L0 edits (new non-normative reference file; pointer additions; CHANGELOG-only edits) remain Lean-eligible and are not a misuse."* The existing Fix (upgrade to Full per `mode-decision-tree.md §Mode upgrade / downgrade`) applies without change.

fixed.title: `.github/scripts/check-version-consistency.sh`
fixed.body: the script's Unreleased policy was inconsistent with `generate-changelog-json.py`. The generator keeps Unreleased in `.releases[]` when it has sections (line 138-141 prune logic: Unreleased is dropped only if empty). The consistency check compared `.releases[0].version` verbatim, which can be the Unreleased entry rather than the latest numbered release. Line 38 already filtered Unreleased out of the CHANGELOG.md comparison; line 41 (before this fix) did not filter the JSON path. Symptom: DRIFT fail whenever a contributor added Unreleased content and regenerated CHANGELOG.json. Fix: changed the jq expression to `[.releases[] | select(.version != "Unreleased")][0].version` making the JSON path symmetric with the MD path. Updated the file-header comment (line 8), report label (line 60), and drift message (line 75) to match the new semantics ("first non-Unreleased release"). Verified against three cases: current state (empty Unreleased, returns 1.17.0), simulated populated Unreleased (returns 1.17.0 via filter), empty `.releases[]` edge case (returns null without crashing).

notes.body: Contributors editing canonical methodology content: check whether the edit is L0 (additive: new reference file, pointer additions, CHANGELOG-only) or L1+ (changing an existing normative claim, adding a new normative rule, renaming a cross-cutting term). L0 remains Lean-eligible; L1+ goes Full per the new row in `mode-decision-tree.md §Scenarios that force Full`.

Contributors who had been working around the `check-version-consistency.sh` drift by avoiding Unreleased entries in PRs: the drift is fixed in this release. The repo's Unreleased-in-PR convention (commit `5aa13e6 revert: drop [Unreleased] CHANGELOG entry from this PR`) still applies as a separate editorial policy; this release does not change that policy, only removes the CI drift that made the policy enforcement feel accidental.
No code migration required. No schema migration required. No manifest field change.

added.title: `reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md` §Shape of a composition → §Invocation lifecycle
added.body: new subsection closing a previously-undocumented mental-model gap: sub-agent calls are **one-shot invocations**, not long-lived processes. The section names the invocation-lifecycle rule (spawn → run → return → terminate), introduces the **invocation handle** concept (some runtimes expose a handle that can seed a new invocation inheriting prior memory), and distinguishes **continuation primitive / state reconstruction** from **state continuation**. Three consequences for the canonical role are enumerated: no cleanup attempts on a returned invocation, no assumed persistence without an explicit continuation call, no dangling handle treated as a pending task. A closing paragraph re-affirms identity is session-bounded — a new invocation seeded from a handle is a new identity for anti-collusion purposes per `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` §Single-agent anti-collusion rule and `§The invariant` above. Prior state: the composition contract was implicitly correct (each canonical-role invocation terminates on return; handoff is manifest-based) but did not name the lifecycle rule — a reader coming from a runtime that exposes an invocation handle could plausibly misread the handle as "the still-running sub-agent," which structurally breaks `§The invariant` identity separation.

added.title: `reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md` §Anti-patterns — two new rows
added.body: covering the concrete runtime-bridge failure modes the new §Invocation lifecycle rule forbids: (1) canonical role attempts cleanup / termination on a sub-agent whose invocation has already returned (wastes tool calls; on runtimes that return a "not found" error, the error further pollutes the canonical role's mental model with a false signal); (2) canonical role treats a stale invocation handle as identity-equivalent to a still-running sub-agent (e.g. reuses the handle for audit work) — a new invocation seeded from a handle is a new identity whose anti-collusion constraints (Rule 5 / Pattern 6) must be re-evaluated, not inherited.

added.title: `docs/glossary.md` §Sub-agent invocation
added.body: new canonical vocabulary term defining the one-shot spawn-run-return cycle, the invocation handle as a continuation primitive (state reconstruction, not state continuation), and the session-bounded identity rule. Placed between §CCKN and §Fan-out as the foundational concept that §Fan-out, §Fan-in, §Parallel group, §Implementation cluster, and §Phase overlap zone all build on. Points at `reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md §Shape of a composition → §Invocation lifecycle` as the canonical reference.

added.title: `AGENTS.md` — `File role map` appendix (`§File role map`)
added.body: classifying every top-level entry file by architectural role: **SoT** (canonical — `AGENTS.md` itself, `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md`, other `docs/*.md`, `skills/**`, `schemas/**` + `templates/**`), **thin-bridge** (`CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `.windsurfrules`, `.cursor/rules/engineering-workflow.mdc`), **runtime role-spec** (`.cursor/rules/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.mdc`, `agents/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.md`), and **reference wrapper** (`reference-implementations/roles/*.md`, `reference-implementations/validator-*/`). Ends with a "Where a new rule belongs" decision guide and the invariant that closes the map: *a normative claim appears in exactly one SoT file; every consumer cites that section by name*. The map is the index the repo was missing — without it, `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` had drifted into treating `agents/implementer.md §Pre-handoff self-check` and `agents/reviewer.md §Anti-rationalization rules` as normative sources of truth, which inverted the intended SoT ↔ bridge relationship.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` §Pre-handoff self-check
added.body: new canonical subsection inside `§Three canonical roles → Implementer` containing the five Implementer questions (acceptance-criterion coverage, verification coverage, reference existence, pattern alignment, evidence-path completion), the "vague / hedged answer is a failing answer" rule, the `implementation_notes` recording protocol, and the Lean / Zero-ceremony mode application. Body text moved verbatim from `agents/implementer.md`; the normative rule now lives with the role that must clear it.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` §Anti-rationalization rules
added.body: new canonical subsection inside `§Three canonical roles → Reviewer` containing the six Reviewer send-back triggers (perfect-confidence hallucination, hedging language, unsubstantiated `pass` entries, read-only review, editing through the back door, thin residual-risk section), the "heuristic failure mirrors" closing note, and the "any one trigger applying is a mandatory send-back, not a judgment call" enforcement clause. Body text moved verbatim from `agents/reviewer.md`.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` TL;DR canonical-contract declaration
added.body: explicitly stating that runtime bridges (`agents/*.md`, `.cursor/rules/*.mdc`, `reference-implementations/roles/*.md`) translate the contract into runtime-specific agent configuration but do not introduce new normative rules — if a bridge states a rule this document does not, the bridge yields. Pointer to `AGENTS.md §File role map` for the full ownership table. Previously the file was de facto canonical but did not self-declare; bridges were free to ship normative content that the contract did not, which is exactly the architecture inversion this release fixes.

added.title: `.github/scripts/check-summary-drift.py`
added.body: pre-release lint that auto-discovers `<number> <noun>` TL;DR claims, counts matching structural entries in the body, and flags mismatches for the noun vocabulary the original 1.14.4 audit caught (axes, anti-patterns, patterns, steps, stages, rules, principles, fracture lines, coordination strategies, hook points). Replaces the one-off `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md` audit artifact (214 lines) with a continuously-enforced CI lint. Wired into `.github/workflows/validate.yml` as the `summary-drift` job. Running against current repo state: 37 docs scanned, no drift.

added.title: `CHANGELOG.archive.md` / `CHANGELOG.archive.json` / `ROADMAP.archive.md`
added.body: pre-1.14.0 releases (1.0.0–1.13.1) and their matching closed initiatives moved to archive files preserving every entry verbatim per `CLAUDE.md §3`. Active files retain Unreleased + 1.14.0 onward and point at the archive. `CHANGELOG.md` shrinks from 465 → 170 lines; `ROADMAP.md` shrinks from 715 → 241 lines. `CHANGELOG.json` regenerated to match the shortened active `CHANGELOG.md`; the full pre-archive snapshot is kept in `CHANGELOG.archive.json`.

changed.title: `agents/implementer.md §Pre-handoff self-check`
changed.body: reduced to a three-sentence pointer to `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md §Pre-handoff self-check` instead of restating the five questions. The body (intro + five questions + recording protocol + mode application) no longer lives here — it lives in the canonical handoff document. The Claude Code sub-agent frontmatter (`tools:` row, `model:`, `description:`) is unchanged; this file remains the runtime bridge that translates the matrix row into a Claude Code sub-agent definition.

changed.title: `agents/reviewer.md §Anti-rationalization rules`
changed.body: reduced to a two-sentence pointer to `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md §Anti-rationalization rules`. The six triggers, the "heuristic failure mirrors" clause, and the enforcement wording all live at the canonical source now; this file no longer restates them.

changed.title: `reference-implementations/roles/implementer.md §Pre-handoff self-check`
changed.body: and **`reference-implementations/roles/reviewer.md §Anti-rationalization rules`** reduced to pointers for the same reason. These paste-ready role prompts were previously holding a second unofficial SoT of the two blocks (verbatim-identical to `agents/*.md` lines 34–46 / 30–41 in their respective files). The new pointer keeps the paste-ready property (the reader still sees the section heading and a one-paragraph "what this is" line) while eliminating the duplicate-SoT drift risk.

changed.title: Consumers of the two moved blocks repointed to canonical SoT
changed.body: per `CLAUDE.md §5` cross-cutting-term discipline: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` (Rule 5a auditable-on-request clause + the anti-metric paragraph on Phase 4 re-entry frequency), `docs/change-manifest-spec.md §parallel_groups` (Reviewer-audits-this-field paragraph), `docs/ai-operating-contract.md §2a Reviewer's sampling right`, `docs/operational-cheat-sheet.md` (Implementer top-5 item 5), `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase4-implement.md` (Pre-handoff self-check section + See-also list), `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/fix-retest-loop.md` (re-entry close-the-loop sentence), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/parallelization-patterns.md` (two rules references in Pattern B + Relationship section), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/cluster-parallelism.md` (per-cluster Implementer self-check bullet + What-this-document-is-not clause), `reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md` (Relationship section). Pointers to `agents/*.md` for content that did not move (Read-manifest-in-full, evidence audit, sampling right, send-back authority) are intentionally left unchanged.

changed.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` reading-order guide (Pair 1, Pair 3)
changed.body: updated to reference local `§Pre-handoff self-check` / `§Anti-rationalization rules` instead of `agents/implementer.md` / `agents/reviewer.md`. The machine-readable pre-filter section's two mentions of `agents/*.md` at `§Pre-filter checks` and `§Hard limits` are likewise updated.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` absorbs the phase × mode matrix
changed.body: . The eight files at `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase-minimums/phase{0..7}-minimums.md` (11–15 lines each, re-classifying the same axes) are replaced by a single `## Phase minimums` section in `SKILL.md` — eight rows × two columns (Lean / Full). `skills/engineering-workflow/README.md` and `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md §How this composes` redirect to the new section.

changed.title: Three mode-selection decision aids repointed to `skills/engineering-workflow/references/mode-decision-tree.md`
changed.body: (the existing execution-layer SoT): `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md §Decision table` (9-row summary table), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/startup-checklist.md §5 Mode selection` (four-bullet mode-criterion summary), and `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md §60-second decision flow` (full Q1–Q4 decision tree) now each are a 1-line pointer at `mode-decision-tree.md`. The forced-Full list divergence resolved in 1.15.0 was the specific failure mode this removes: three parallel copies of the same list inevitably drift.

removed.title: `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md`
removed.body: (214 lines) — superseded by `.github/scripts/check-summary-drift.py`. The audit was a one-off meta-record produced 2026-04-22; all eight (a) real-drift findings were resolved in 1.14.4 + the one cross-reference finding in 1.14.5. Historical CHANGELOG / ROADMAP references to the file (in the archived 1.14.4 / 1.14.5 entries) are preserved verbatim per `CLAUDE.md §3`.

changed.title: Four execution modes are now first-class citizens of the glossary.
changed.body: Prior to this release, `docs/principles.md §8` named four modes ("no-process path, three-line delivery, Lean, Full") but the execution layer (`SKILL.md`, `mode-decision-tree.md`, `startup-checklist.md`, `glossary.md`) only defined Lean and Full. The missing two were referenced in other files under five different names (`no-process path`, `ultra-lean`, `No skill`, `three-line handoff`, `stripped-down version` for the lowest mode; `three-line handoff` vs `three-line delivery` for the gray-zone mode), with no single canonical home. `docs/glossary.md §Execution mode` now defines all four — **Zero-ceremony, Three-line delivery, Lean, Full** — as the normative names, with per-mode artifact minimums, selection criteria, and an explicit "legacy aliases (retired)" note mapping the five old names to their canonical equivalents. The glossary is the SoT; other files cite it rather than redefining.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/mode-decision-tree.md` rewritten
changed.body: to be the execution-layer source of truth for mode selection. It now has five scenario tables — forced-Full, forced-Lean, forced-Three-line-delivery, forced-Zero-ceremony, and mode upgrade/downgrade triggers — covering all four modes. Prior versions only distinguished forced-Full vs forced-Lean. Forced-Full triggers are consolidated into a single canonical list (DB migration / public API breaking / enum consumer-visible / payments / auth / PII / cross-team handoff / long-lived feature-flag / staged rollout); earlier the list was split across `mode-decision-tree.md` and `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md §Q3` with mismatches (`enum` + `rollout` appeared only in the onboarding doc; `cross-team` + `long-lived flag` + `PII` appeared only in the decision tree). Other decision aids (`SKILL.md §Decision table`, `startup-checklist.md §5`, `when-not-to-use-this.md §60-second decision flow`) now cite this file rather than carry parallel lists.

changed.title: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` gains a master ceremony-scaling table
changed.body: at the top of the six-rules section, spelling out how each of Rules 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 / 5a / 6 applies in each of the four execution modes. Previously only Rule 5a had explicit ceremony scaling (`Zero-ceremony waived / Lean once / Full every boundary`); the other rules were written as if they applied uniformly, implying a Lean bugfix still needed six named gates plus six commits plus a ROADMAP row per step. The master table makes the real scaling explicit: **Lean mode compresses all six rules into a single gate event at Lean-5**, **Three-line delivery waives phase-gate discipline entirely** (the commit message is the record), and **Zero-ceremony is fully waived**. Rule 5a's existing scaling block is preserved and extended to include the Three-line-delivery row. The "single P0 + P7 pair" sentence for single-change initiatives is scoped to Full mode, resolving a contradiction with `AGENTS.md §6`'s previous "Trivial single-phase tasks are exempt from the ROADMAP rule" wording.

changed.title: `AGENTS.md §6`
changed.body: updated to state the five rules' Full-mode-only application scope up-front, then point at the phase-gate master ceremony-scaling table. Evidence-before-completion remains universal (every mode). The previous exemption sentence ("Trivial single-phase tasks are exempt") is replaced with the four-mode scaling clause that references the canonical ceremony-scaling table.

changed.title: `docs/principles.md §8`
changed.body: updated to use the canonical mode names (Zero-ceremony / Three-line delivery / Lean / Full) and point at `glossary.md §Execution mode` and `mode-decision-tree.md` as the canonical sources rather than standing alone.

changed.title: `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md`
changed.body: updated to use canonical mode names (Zero-ceremony instead of "No-process path"; Three-line delivery instead of "stripped-down version" / "three-line handoff"). The 60-second decision flow now covers all four modes explicitly (Q1 → Zero-ceremony, Q2 → Zero-ceremony, Q3 → forced-Full check, Q4 → Three-line delivery vs Lean). The edge-case quick-reference table uses canonical names. The forced-Full trigger list is delegated to `mode-decision-tree.md` rather than duplicated.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md`
changed.body: updated: the frontmatter description lists four modes instead of two. The `§Mode selection` block has four sub-sections (Zero-ceremony / Three-line delivery / Lean / Full) with per-mode artifact minimums. The `§Decision table` adds rows for Zero-ceremony and Three-line delivery; the ghost term "ultra-lean" (referenced but never defined anywhere in the repo) is removed. The `§Lean workflow` section now explicitly notes Lean-0 … Lean-5 are **steps, not phases** — phase-gate discipline applies once at Lean-5, not per step — and points at the `glossary.md §Lean → Full step / phase correspondence` mapping table for upgrade cases. The `§Artifact guidance` section adds Zero-ceremony and Three-line-delivery rows.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/startup-checklist.md §5 and §6`
changed.body: rewritten to cover all four modes. §5 now explicitly lists each mode's selection criterion; §6 lists each mode's artifact minimum. Both point at `mode-decision-tree.md` as the selection SoT and `phase-gate-discipline.md §Ceremony scaling` for rule applicability.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/misuse-signals.md`
changed.body: §Misuse 1 and §Misuse 2 rewritten to cover downgrading to / upgrading from any of the four modes, not just Lean↔Full. Each misuse block now references `mode-decision-tree.md` as the authority for the correct mode and `glossary.md §Lean → Full step / phase correspondence` for Lean↔Full transitions.

changed.title: `AGENTS.md`
changed.body: top-of-file description and `skills/engineering-workflow/README.md`** feature list updated** to name the four execution modes explicitly instead of "Lean / Full modes".

added.title: `docs/glossary.md §Lean → Full step / phase correspondence`
added.body: a new mapping table documenting which Full-mode phase each Lean-mode step corresponds to, so that Lean→Full upgrades mid-change re-enter at the correct Full phase mechanically. Prior to this release, mode-upgrade procedure in `mode-decision-tree.md` said "re-enter from the current phase" but did not specify which Full phase a Lean step maps to — Lean-2 Minimal Plan merges Full's Phase 2 Plan + Phase 3 Test Plan, Lean-4 Verify covers portions of Full's Phase 5 Review + Phase 6 Sign-off, and Lean has no Phase 8 counterpart, so the mapping was implicit.

fixed.title: Ghost term `ultra-lean`
fixed.body: in `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md §Decision table` removed. The term was used in the decision table (`"Tiny single-file fix, no public behavior impact | No skill or ultra-lean"`) but had no definition anywhere in the repo. Replaced with canonical mode names (Zero-ceremony).

fixed.title: Numbering-ambiguity source:
fixed.body: AGENTS.md §6's previous "Trivial single-phase tasks (Lean-mode bugfixes, typos) are exempt from the ROADMAP rule" did not reconcile with `SKILL.md §Lean workflow`'s Lean-0 … Lean-5 six-step structure or with `phase-gate-discipline.md:225`'s "single P0 + P7 pair" requirement for single-change initiatives. The three documents now agree: Lean mode has **steps** (six of them) that compress into a **single phase-gate event** at Lean-5 delivery; ROADMAP rows are optional for a Lean-mode single-change initiative; the P0 + P7 minimum applies to Full-mode single-change initiatives only.

fixed.title: Forced-Full trigger list divergence
fixed.body: between `mode-decision-tree.md` and `when-not-to-use-this.md §Q3`. Previously `enum` + `rollout` appeared only in the onboarding doc and `cross-team handoff` + `long-lived feature-flag` + `PII` appeared only in the decision tree; the consolidated list now contains all of them (DB migration, public API breaking change, enum consumer-visible, payments, auth/PII, cross-team handoff, long-lived feature-flag, staged rollout) and is maintained in `mode-decision-tree.md` only, with `when-not-to-use-this.md` citing it.

fixed.title: Orphan-mode problem:
fixed.body: `Three-line delivery` was defined in `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md` and named in `docs/principles.md §8`, but did not appear in `SKILL.md`, `mode-decision-tree.md`, `startup-checklist.md`, or `glossary.md`. An agent walking only the execution layer would never select this mode. Now present and normative across all execution-layer decision aids.

notes.body: Repositories that were writing per-Lean-step gates + per-Lean-step commits + per-Lean-step ROADMAP rows were over-applying Full-mode discipline to Lean-mode work; they may stop. No artifact rewrites are required — over-ceremony is not a correctness problem, only an efficiency one.

References to `no-process path`, `ultra-lean`, `No skill`, `three-line handoff`, and `stripped-down version` in project-local documents remain readable; `glossary.md §Execution mode` maps each to its canonical name. New local documents should use the canonical names.

Bridge and example files carrying the legacy `Zero ceremony` phrasing in their "Situation | Path" tables are left as-is per `CLAUDE.md §3` (factual record preservation for historical content); the glossary's retired-aliases note makes them readable.

fixed.title: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md:95` — broken relative reference to `templates/lean-spec-template.md`.
fixed.body: Rule 5a's Ceremony scaling block pointed at `templates/lean-spec-template.md`; the file actually lives at `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/lean-spec-template.md`. Under either reading convention (relative-to-file → `docs/templates/...`; repo-root → `templates/...`) the prior path did not resolve. Path corrected to `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/lean-spec-template.md`, matching this file's existing repo-root-path convention for all its other cross-reference links (`skills/engineering-workflow/references/resumption-protocol.md`, `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase-minimums/*`, etc.). Discovered during the summary-vs-per-case audit on 2026-04-22 and recorded as a non-summary-vs-per-case finding in `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md`; now resolved.

fixed.title: `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md` — registry updated.
fixed.body: The "Other drift" entry for the `phase-gate-discipline.md:95` broken reference receives a `**Resolved in 1.14.5** (2026-04-23)` note naming the correction and confirming the fix scope is isolated (the other `templates/change-manifest.example-*.yaml` references in `docs/` do resolve correctly against the repo-root `templates/` directory, so no accompanying edits were needed).

fixed.title: `docs/source-of-truth-patterns.md:4` — A1: anti-pattern count.
fixed.body: TL;DR "*Five common desync anti-patterns*" corrected to "*Six common desync anti-patterns*" with the six named inline (dual-write, consumer-derived truth, stale cache, doc drift, i18n key drift, pipeline-order as implementation detail). Anti-pattern 6 pairs with sub-pattern 4a Pipeline-Order Contract; the row was added when 4a landed but the TL;DR was never updated. TL;DR also clarifies that half-sync gotchas are a *callout within Pattern 8*, not a separate pattern.

fixed.title: `docs/implementation-disciplines.md:4` — A2: axis list mismatch.
fixed.body: TL;DR claimed six axes including two phantom ones ("concurrency + lifecycle", "dependency + environment") that do not exist as axes in the body; the body has five axes including "Documentation sync" that the TL;DR omitted. TL;DR rewritten to name the five actual axes (Contract clarity, Clear layering, Data-access health, Errors and observability, Documentation sync) and to point at `cross-cutting-concerns.md` as the real home of concurrency / cancellation propagation (error-handling sub-section) and dependency / environment concerns (build-time-risk). Reader following the TL;DR is now guided to the correct location for each concern.

fixed.title: `docs/user-surface-disciplines.md:4` — A3: axis list mismatch.
fixed.body: TL;DR claimed six axes (flow correctness, contract alignment, feedback + guidance, a11y, responsive + device, analytics + telemetry); body has five with different names (UI flow correctness, Contract alignment, Internationalization and copy, Usability, Operational hooks). Only 2 of 6 TL;DR axes matched the body verbatim. TL;DR rewritten to name the five actual axes — a11y and responsive are now correctly folded under "Usability" (as the body organizes them), and "Internationalization and copy" surfaces as its own axis (the TL;DR previously demoted i18n to a sub-bullet).

fixed.title: `docs/adoption-strategy.md:4` — A4: stage-duration drift.
fixed.body: TL;DR said "Stage 2 (1-2 months) ... Stage 3 (3+ months)"; body and `docs/diagrams.md:161-162` say 2-4 weeks and 4-8 weeks. TL;DR corrected to match body (2-4 weeks / 4-8 weeks). Adopting teams planning capacity against these durations no longer hit a 2–3× error in the TL;DR.

fixed.title: `docs/ci-cd-integration-hooks.md:4` — A5: Hook 6 description wrong.
fixed.body: TL;DR said Hook 6 was "Change Manifest schema validation"; body §Hook 6 is titled "completion-report check". The actual schema-validation work lives in `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md` Layer 1, not in this document's hook list. TL;DR corrected: Hook 6 now reads "pre-merge → completion-report check (Full-mode PRs)"; Hook 7 tightened from "observation-window start" to "Phase 8 observation reminder" to match the body. TL;DR also adds a clarifying sentence that schema validation is the automation contract's concern, so the boundary between this doc and the algorithm doc is explicit.

fixed.title: `docs/concurrent-changes.md:4` — A6: coordination pattern mismatch.
fixed.body: TL;DR said "Three coordination patterns: sequential, parallel with shared branch, dual-write bridge"; body §Coordination strategies has **four** strategies with different names (sequencing, branch isolation + merge-time conflict resolution, joint design + split implementation, merge into one ticket). "Dual-write bridge" does not appear in the body at all — the dual-write mechanism is a `breaking-change-framework.md` Path B concern, not a coordination strategy in this doc. TL;DR rewritten to name the four actual strategies.

fixed.title: `docs/system-change-perspective.md:4` + line 9 — A7: "six unified questions" is a phantom.
fixed.body: TL;DR claimed this file "lays out the six unified questions" and the body blockquote (line 8–9) claimed the list "lives in `docs/surfaces.md`". Neither is true: `surfaces.md` has "six cross-cutting concerns" (different thing — line 200) and a "60-second opener" with **five** questions (different content). Both claims removed. TL;DR now describes the **four surface-entry questions** the body actually lays out (inside §Don't start by asking "whose layer?") and points at `surfaces.md §60-second opener` for the full five-question analysis checklist (which does exist there). Body blockquote correspondingly updated to reference "§60-second opener" instead of "six unified analysis questions".

fixed.title: `docs/change-decomposition.md:4` — A8: fracture-line under-reporting.
fixed.body: TL;DR named four fracture lines (SoT, surface, consumer pace, risk asymmetry); body §Natural fracture lines has six (SoT boundary, surface cadence differs, consumer cohort separation, asymmetric risk level, different reversibility, delivery-sequencing constraint). TL;DR expanded to all six, plus the four merge signals (tightly-coupled atomicity, shared evidence, split would fabricate a scheduling dependency, changes are too small) that were previously unnamed in the TL;DR. Reader using the TL;DR now sees the full decomposition decision surface instead of missing the last two fracture criteria.

changed.title: `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md`
changed.body: updated to mark all eight (a) findings as **Resolved in 1.14.4** with a per-finding resolution note. The registry is kept intact as a dated audit record — findings are not deleted, because the registry's own "don't silently re-ship" rule requires resolution to be visible, not erased. The registry's (b) legitimate two-level classifications and (c) boundary-call classifications remain unchanged.

notes.body: Audit produced: `0d8928f docs(audit): summary-vs-per-case consistency registry` (2026-04-22, not a release)

Resolution: **this commit** (`chore(release): 1.14.4`, 2026-04-22) Future audits should follow the same two-commit shape (audit artifact first, resolution release second) so reviewers can separately review (a) the finding list and (b) the chosen resolution direction. Bundling both into a single commit would hide which findings existed before the edits.

fixed.title: `docs/operational-cheat-sheet.md:60` and `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md:98` — stale "Stage 2" markers removed.
fixed.body: Both rows carried a "*(pending Stage 2)*" / "*(CCKN, once Stage 2 lands)*" qualifier that framed CCKN as a not-yet-shipped feature. The qualifier refers to an internal 1.7.x-era "Stage 2 schema changes" roadmap phase (per the `bebd9c3` commit message "*the agent-protocol-vs-SDD adoption plan*"); the CCKN schema field + canonical doc shipped in the **immediately following** commit `596e5bb` on 2026-04-21, one minute after the markers were written. The markers have therefore been stale from the moment they landed — for 14 patch/minor releases through 1.8.0 → 1.14.2. Both rows now point directly at `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` without the "pending" qualifier. Root cause of the miss: 1.14.0 / 1.14.1 / 1.14.2 all scoped their CCKN reviews to the canonical file + three named consumers (`glossary.md`, `phase1-investigate.md`, `SKILL.md`); the full list of CCKN-mentioning files (10) was not swept until the post-1.14.2 user-requested audit. The two fixes are independent of the 1.14.0–1.14.2 normative content — they are pre-existing documentation drift that those releases' scope did not cover.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` §What to do on no match — residual contradiction with the 1.14.1 summary rule
fixed.body: is corrected. 1.14.1 rewrote the §When to query summary to say "*write during Phase 1 — at initial Investigate per §Ceremony scaling, or at re-entry after a Phase 4 Discovery loop*", but left the §What to do on no match paragraph saying "*At the end of the change, the Planner evaluates ... — that is the write-side rule*." A reader arriving via the "No match" flow therefore still saw end-of-change framed as *the* write point, directly contradicting the corrected summary. The same wording was mirrored in `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase1-investigate.md` §Startup: query CCKN precedent "No match" bullet. Both are rewritten to make the timing explicit: on no match, a write only happens if Phase 1 itself surfaces reusable knowledge (write during this Phase 1 per §Ceremony scaling) or if a Phase 4 Discovery loop later triggers Phase 1 re-entry (write at re-entry per §Relation to the Change Manifest §3). Otherwise the change produces no CCKN. Root cause: 1.14.1's edit scope was the summary sentence; the downstream prose that carried the same framing was not re-scanned.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` §Ceremony scaling — query vs write responsibilities per ceremony tier
fixed.body: is now explicit. 1.14.0 / 1.14.1 shipped §When to query (query side) and §Ceremony scaling (write side) as adjacent but logically disconnected sections — neither stated whether Lean mode shares the Phase 1 startup query or whether the Full-mode evaluation in §Ceremony scaling also determines *when to write*. 1.14.1's summary sentence "*at initial Investigate per §Ceremony scaling*" cross-referenced a section that only said "*evaluates during Phase 1*" — a semi-circular pointer. The rewritten §Ceremony scaling now: (a) opens with a one-line rule separating query (always on in non-Zero-ceremony tiers) from write (tier-scaled); (b) explicitly states Zero-ceremony skips both query and write; (c) Lean mode runs the query, write is optional, with write-timing tied to Phase 1 (initial or re-entry) rather than end-of-change; (d) Full mode states both evaluation *and* the two possible write timings (available-at-Phase-1 write immediately, otherwise defer to re-entry after Phase 4 Discovery).

fixed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Investigate phase block — SKILL.md grep-target de-duplication is now complete.
fixed.body: 1.14.1 removed the inline match-handling restatement from SKILL.md but left the grep-target sentence ("*grep the repo's CCKN directory (default: `docs/knowledge/`) for `topics` overlap with anticipated `surfaces_touched`, any `uncontrolled_interfaces`, or libraries / third-party APIs / platforms the change will touch*") duplicated verbatim with the same sentence in `cross-change-knowledge.md:111-115` (canonical) and `phase1-investigate.md:24` (procedural bridge). Three consumer copies of the grep target is exactly the drift pattern 1.14.1 invoked `CLAUDE.md §5` to forbid — the de-duplication was partial. The paragraph is now a one-sentence discoverability cue plus explicit pointers at the procedural bridge and the canonical rule; the grep target, the three overlap axes, and all match-handling now live only at the canonical + procedural-bridge layer. A future change to the grep target touches two files instead of three.

fixed.title: `CHANGELOG.md` 1.14.0 §Why minor — forward correction
fixed.body: of the miscited claim "*Lean-mode changes (where CCKN query is optional per §Ceremony scaling) all remain valid*." §Ceremony scaling (pre-1.14.2) addressed the CCKN **write** side only — it never carved out a "Lean-mode query is optional" rule, and the new §When to query rule (1.14.0) did not exempt Lean either. The 1.14.0 entry stays intact per `CLAUDE.md §3` ("*CHANGELOG is a factual record*"); this bullet documents the correction forward. The actual rule post-1.14.2: the Phase 1 startup query runs in both Lean and Full modes; only the write side is ceremony-scaled. Zero-ceremony continues to skip both.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` §When to query a CCKN — summary rule
fixed.body: corrected from "*write at Phase 4 Discovery / Phase 1 re-entry; query at Phase 1 Investigate startup*" (the 1.14.0 wording) to "*query at Phase 1 Investigate startup; write during Phase 1 — at initial Investigate per §Ceremony scaling, or at re-entry after a Phase 4 Discovery loop per §Relation to the Change Manifest §3. Phase 4 Discovery triggers re-entry; it does not itself write.*" The 1.14.0 summary implied two write locations (Phase 4 Discovery **and** Phase 1 re-entry) and directly contradicted the pre-existing §Relation to the Change Manifest §3 rule that writing happens "*as part of the Phase 1 Investigate re-entry, **not as a side effect of Phase 4***." A reader following the 1.14.0 summary would conclude Phase 4 is a valid write location, which §3 forbids. Root cause: the summary collapsed the trigger source (Phase 4 Discovery) and the write execution point (Phase 1 re-entry) into a single slash-joined phrase without distinguishing them.

fixed.title: `docs/glossary.md` CCKN entry — asymmetric-timing paragraph
fixed.body: receives the same correction so the vocabulary-layer summary matches the canonical reference.

fixed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase1-investigate.md` §Startup: query CCKN precedent
fixed.body: gains the missing **partial match** row (CCKN covers part of the relevant facet → cite for covered + investigate uncovered + extend CCKN if generalizing knowledge emerges). The 1.14.0 phase-file procedural bridge listed only fresh / stale / no match, dropping the "partial match" handling that the canonical §When to query a CCKN lists. An agent following the phase file alone would not learn that partial coverage still requires CCKN extension — which is load-bearing for write-side discipline. The same section also gains a one-sentence clarification that an absent CCKN directory is treated as no match, not as a failure (removes the risk of a literal `grep` erroring on a non-existent directory and being misread as a tooling failure).

fixed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Investigate phase block
fixed.body: reduces the bolded "Startup: query CCKN precedent" paragraph from a full match-handling restatement to a single-sentence pointer at the phase file and the canonical reference. The 1.14.0 version restated fresh / stale / no-match inline, creating **two consumer copies** of the match-handling rule (SKILL.md plus the phase file) — exactly the drift pattern `CLAUDE.md §5` warns against. The reduced pointer keeps the surface-layer discoverability (an agent reading SKILL.md's Investigate block still sees the rule exists) while leaving the phase file as the single procedural source of truth.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` §At Phase 1 Investigate startup
fixed.body: gains the same directory-absent clarification ("an absent directory is not a failure") so the canonical reference and the phase-file bridge agree on the edge case.

added.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` §When to query a CCKN
added.body: new section between §Relation to the Change Manifest and §What a CCKN is not, closing the long-standing **query-side timing gap** in the 1.8.0 CCKN pattern. Prior state: CCKN's **write** side was prescribed ("*the Planner creates or extends a CCKN as part of the Phase 1 Investigate re-entry*" / "*the Planner evaluates during Phase 1 Investigate whether the change's learning content belongs in a CCKN*") but the **query** side was only passively mentioned ("*Any manifest field that benefits from pre-existing knowledge cites the CCKN by path*") — no named procedural step said "query first, before investigation proper begins." Result: agents re-discovered facts that a CCKN would have supplied, and the write-side infrastructure sat unused on the query side. The new §When to query defines the **asymmetric lifecycle rule**: **write at Phase 4 Discovery / Phase 1 re-entry; query at Phase 1 Investigate startup**. Section covers: (1) Phase 1 startup grep against `docs/knowledge/` for `topics` overlap with `surfaces_touched` / `uncontrolled_interfaces` / libraries-APIs-platforms-touched; (2) match-handling (fresh = cite + skip re-discovery; stale = cite + inherit refresh obligation per §Not a free pass for stale claims + record in `knowledge_notes_touched[]`; partial = cite for covered portion + investigate the rest); (3) no-match fall-through with the existing write-side rule as the end-of-change checkpoint; (4) 5 anti-patterns including the primary one (querying mid-implementation instead of at Phase 1 startup — decisions the CCKN should have informed have already been made).

added.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase1-investigate.md` — new "Startup: query CCKN precedent" section
added.body: terse (~8-line) procedural step positioned before "Investigation dimensions," pointing at the full §When to query rule in the canonical reference. Names the three match outcomes (fresh / stale / no match) and the primary anti-pattern (late querying). Keeps the phase-file terse style (~50 lines total including this addition).

changed.title: `docs/glossary.md` — CCKN entry
changed.body: gains an asymmetric-lifecycle paragraph: "*write at Phase 4 Discovery / Phase 1 re-entry; query at Phase 1 Investigate startup*." Surfaces the timing distinction at the vocabulary layer so a reader finding "Cross-Change Knowledge Note" in another document knows immediately both when to write and when to query, without having to read the full canonical reference to discover the query side was previously absent.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Investigate phase block
changed.body: gains a bolded "Startup: query CCKN precedent" paragraph with the same grep-target / match-handling summary. The skill-layer quick reference now surfaces the rule at the point an agent is deciding Investigate-phase capability priorities, not only when it reads the full phase file.
